  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The user explicitly requests checking whether the reported results (e_ss and GM in Table 2) substantiate the stated claims about tracking and stability.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment should surface limits or uncertainty in how conclusively the provided metrics support the claims.",
    "RX.2": "We must avoid endorsing overgeneralized conclusions beyond what the single reported trace and metrics justify.",
    "RX.3": "The work must stay within verifying claim-evidence linkage and not drift into redesigning experiments or broader critique.",

  }

rationale.R1.3: The user explicitly requests checking whether the reported results (e_ss and GM in Table 2) substantiate the stated claims about tracking and stability.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should surface limits or uncertainty in how conclusively the provided metrics support the claims.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid endorsing overgeneralized conclusions beyond what the single reported trace and metrics justify.
rationale.RX.3: The work must stay within verifying claim-evidence linkage and not drift into redesigning experiments or broader critique.
rationale.RX.4: Guarding against overstated conclusions helps prevent potential downstream harm from misguided deployment decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Judgments should be traceable to specific elements of the artifact (definitions and Table 2 values) for auditability.
